title: Revision History panel DOM structure anchors the selectors needed to theme its internals
content:
The Revision History panel (opened by the toolbar history button) has a stable internal structure that CSS selectors can rely on when theming it.

Root: `#document-editor-context[data-editor][data-document-type]`

Inside that, the tree is:

- `div.p-6` — main container
  - `header` — top bar
    - `button[name="close-revision-history"]` — back arrow
    - `h1` — document title
    - `button.bg-slate-50` — DOC ID pill
    - `button.bg-slate-50` — BRANCH pill
    - `div[id="scope select"]` — scope dropdown
  - `div.rounded-md.bg-slate-50` — timeline content area
    - `div.border-slate-100` — timeline with left border
      - `h2.bg-slate-50` — day separator (e.g. "Changes on Mar 26")
      - `article.bg-white` — revision card, repeated per revision
        - RevisionNumber, Operation, Timestamp, Signature, Errors rows
  - `div` — pagination (if needed)

Key selector anchors for theming:
- `.bg-slate-50` — header pills and day separators
- `.rounded-md.bg-slate-50` — timeline content area background
- `.border-slate-100` — timeline left border
- `article` — revision cards (bg-white by default)
- `.text-gray-900`, `.text-stone-300`, `.text-gray-600` — text color tiers
- `.border-gray-200`, `.bg-white` — card borders and backgrounds

Scope all overrides to `[data-document-type="your/document-type"]` to avoid bleeding into other editors.
description: The Revision History panel has a fixed internal DOM tree under #document-editor-context — header pills, timeline, day separators, article revision cards — that CSS rules target by class.
